rulings.Case Title: ENTERLINE VS ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(BHC)*
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 24, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant fails to shift the burden on all causes of action except for negligence. Plaintiff creates a triable issue of fact as to exposure to defendant's products. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are overruled. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: US PARKING INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL et al

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS, INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Pltf'S Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Under The Anti Slapp Statute (Ccp 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 24, 2009, line 12, RESPONDENT HAPPENING HOUSE VENTURES, and DAVID SMITH'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Under The Anti Slapp Statute (Ccp 425.16). Motion denied. The gravamen of the third and fourth causes of action is that defendant mismanaged plaintiff and Happening House Ventures and engaged in self-dealings. The allegation that are subject of this motion are incidental to those causes of action. Request for fees denied. The motion was not frivolous. =(302/CWWPB)
